title: Magnetic Effect of a Current — Easy

questions.question: What is the shape of the magnetic field lines around a long, straight current-carrying wire?
options: Concentric circles centred on the wire
Straight lines parallel to the wire
Radial lines pointing away from the wire
An elliptical loop around the ends of the wire
questions.explanation:
According to the sources, the magnetic field due to a current in a long straight wire consists of concentric circles centred on the wire.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: What is the effect on the magnetic field around a wire if the direction of the electric current is reversed?
options: The direction of the magnetic field lines is reversed
The strength of the magnetic field increases
The shape of the field lines changes to squares
questions.explanation:
The direction of the current affects the direction of the magnetic field; reversing the current reverses the direction of the field lines.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which component consists of a long coil of wire that produces a magnetic field similar to that of a bar magnet when current flows through it?
questions.explanation:
A solenoid is a coil of wire that, when carrying current, produces a magnetic field pattern very similar to a bar magnet.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: How can the direction of the magnetic field around a straight wire be determined if the direction of the current is known?
questions.explanation:
The right-hand grip rule is the standard method to relate current direction (thumb) to magnetic field direction (fingers) for a straight wire.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which of these devices specifically uses the magnetic effect of a current to operate?
questions.explanation:
Relays and loudspeakers are explicitly listed as devices that use the magnetic effect of a current.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
